round1: Round 1 is in the books, and while everyone correctly predicted Tampa Bay’s dominant sweep in 5, the rest of your picks were mostly a masterclass in mediocrity. Chris, it seems you actually watched hockey, unlike the rest of you who collectively proved that picking against Calgary, Montreal, or Philadelphia was just asking for trouble. And Stephanie, picking Ottawa? Seriously?
round2: Round 2 is in the books, and some of you actually paid attention. Stephanie nailed Tampa's sweep, and Dad and Marc correctly picked Philly in 6. The rest of you were mostly guessing, especially those who thought Colorado or Toronto stood a chance, or that Detroit wouldn't get sent packing by Calgary.
round3: Calgary and Tampa Bay advance, though Jaclyn's Flyers pick was as accurate as Stephanie's five-game predictions. At least Dad and Jaclyn got the Flames series dead on, showing some of you pay attention.
round4: Well, Round 4 is over, and Tampa Bay took the Cup in 7 games. Not a single one of you picked the Lightning, proving collective delusion is alive and well, though Mom at least nailed the game count. Jaclyn, picking a Calgary sweep, must have been watching a different series entirely.
overall: Alright, the 2004 NHL playoffs are officially in the books, and while the Lightning barely squeaked out a Cup, our own Derrick absolutely dominated this pool, cruising to victory with a commanding 50 points on the back of 10 solid team picks and six correct game counts. Chrissy put up a valiant effort, finishing a respectable second with nine teams and an impressive eight game counts correct, proving she's almost as good as Derrick but not quite. Jaclyn and Marc landed squarely in the mediocre middle, both picking nine teams and four game counts correctly, which is fine, I guess, if you're into participation trophies. Meanwhile, Stephanie, bless her heart, managed to secure the undisputed title of pool loser with a dismal 27 points, only managing to pick eight teams and a pathetic three game counts right. Honestly, Stephanie, your picks were so bad, it's a miracle you even knew hockey was a thing.
